methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Classiq

data.narrative:
Riverlane is a Series C quantum computing company founded by Steve Brierley that has raised $120M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comps (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private comps including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using the $120M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $204M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow quantum computing sector—only 7 same-sector peers exist, but filtering for stage and valuation tier fails to produce the 3+ datapoints required for reliable median calculation. Using an all-sector median would introduce material bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $173M to $235M, with a median of $204M. This range reflects a single applicable method, creating meaningful uncertainty around the point estimate. Key risks to valuation include the highly nascent and fragmented nature of quantum computing markets, capital intensity of the sector, and stage-progression methodology's reliance on historical step-up patterns that may not hold in frontier technology. The absence of viable peer multiples underscores that public market comparables in quantum computing remain limited anchors for private-stage pricing.
